articles.title: [Editorial] Protecting childhood: a pressing priority
articles.summary: All children deserve to grow up in safe, stable, and nurturing environments. Yet millions globally face adversity, with their youngest years marked by stress, trauma, or abuse. So-called adverse childhood experiences (ACEs) include sexual, physical, or emotional abuse, physical or emotional neglect, and parental separation or divorce. Witnessing intimate partner violence, living with someone who has a mental illness or substance misuse problem, or having a household member who is incarcerated are further ACEs.

articles.title: Hong Kong school rearranges overnight World Cup watch party after feedback
articles.summary:
A Hong Kong primary school that earlier suggested hosting an overnight World Cup viewing event has watered down the plan and opted for a weekend morning timeslot in response to feedback from parents.
The school clarified that its initial circular, which slated the event for the very early hours of July 15, sought to gauge parental interest, while its shift to daytime aimed to allow for more parents and students to watch the match together.
In a statement on Wednesday – about a week after the...

articles.title: Real Estate, Reinvented: How Alexis Zaballa and One Trade S.A. Let Everyday Investors Own a Piece of a Wyndham Hotel
articles.summary: One Trade S.A., founded by Alexis Zaballa, is revolutionizing real estate investment in Latin America through a "condohotel" model where individual investors can own a hotel room operated by Wyndham Hotels & Resorts. This model offers a fixed 8% annual return, contractually guaranteed for 15 years, without the operational burdens typically associated with property ownership. The company has already launched one hotel in Paraguay and is developing five more across Paraguay, the Dominican Republic, and Argentina, aiming to make hotel ownership accessible to everyday investors with entry points around $95,000.

articles.title: Widepoint COO Todd Dzyak sells $150,000 in company stock
articles.summary: Widepoint COO Todd Dzyak sold 10,000 shares of company stock for $150,000 at $15.00 per share. This sale occurred while the stock was near its 52-week high after a 335% surge over the past year, and InvestingPro analysis suggests it is currently overvalued. Dzyak still holds 135,436 shares and options for an additional 9,714 shares.

articles.title: Ask HN: How do you test AI-generated code?
articles.summary:
When AI generates code, I first instruct the model to find, fix, and verify any issues. After that, I start the server and test whether it actually works from the user’s perspective.

What I’m looking for is a workflow where issues are received, fixed, tested, and deployed—but it seems that current AI agents aren’t very good at performing browser tests from the user’s perspective.

I’ve tried using the built-in browsers in Codex and Cursor, but they often only checked whether the page loaded. In the end, I had to instruct them step by step on what to do, and it turned out to be cheaper and faster for me to test it myself.

So I’m curious to know how you’ve set up test automation. Are there any services that do this (for individuals, not just enterprises)? If you’re using a harness like Codex, I’d like to know what instructions and skills are needed to get it to perform tests from the user’s perspective.

articles.summary:

The machine at the centre of the discussion is not a laptop-sized piece of equipment that can be easily packed into a shipping carton. ASML’s EUV lithography systems weigh about 180 tonnes, contain a vast number of precision components, are shipped to customers in...

articles.title: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals to Present Data from First-in-Human, Dose-Ranging Phase 1 LUMINA Trial of AMX0114 in ALS at ENCALS 2026
articles.summary: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ: AMLX) announced that it will present data from its Phase 1 LUMINA trial of AMX0114 for ALS at the ENCALS Meeting 2026. The initial data from Cohort 1 showed AMX0114 had no drug-related serious adverse events and no serious neurological adverse events, supporting continued evaluation at higher doses. The LUMINA study is a multinational, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial evaluating the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of AMX0114 in people with ALS, and Cohorts 1 and 2 are fully enrolled, with Cohort 3 currently enrolling.

articles.title: Is Kraft Heinz (KHC) Turning Regional Reshuffle Into a Coherent Brand and Execution Strategy?
articles.summary: Kraft Heinz recently reorganized its global operations into three regions and launched new products, aiming to refresh brands and sharpen execution after mixed performance. This article explores how the new structure, especially the consolidation of Emerging Markets, might affect its investment narrative, which hinges on brand relevance, margin protection, and stable earnings. While some see potential for growth in emerging markets, others remain cautious about innovation and consumer preference shifts, especially in the North American market.

articles.title: PLDT data center REIT listing plan seen as net credit positive
articles.summary: CreditSights views PLDT Inc.'s planned P24.2-billion digital infrastructure real estate investment trust (REIT) listing for its data center business as a net credit positive, despite maintaining an "underperform" recommendation on the company. The proceeds from the REIT listing are expected to significantly reduce PLDT's P284.7 billion net debt and support the expansion of its data center portfolio. The listing is projected to bring net leverage to around 2.7x-2.8x by year-end.

articles.title: Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison
articles.summary:
Israel denying Palestinian women medical care in prison

Palestinian women are facing “catastrophic” conditions in Israel’s Damon Prison, according to a report by the Palestinian Prisoners and Ex-Prisoners Affairs Commission. 

The Commission cited severe overcrowding, deliberate medical neglect, and the denial of basic human rights.

Damon Prison, which currently holds 87 Palestinian women, is also reportedly denying pregnant prisoners necessary media care and basic necessities. 

The Commission added that women are only allowed outside their cells for one hour per day for recreation and showering, and face poor-quality food and shortages of clothing, personal belongings, and hygiene products.

articles.title: S&P shakeup adds Honeywell Aerospace, Toast and NHI
articles.summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices announced several index changes taking effect between June 29 and July 1, 2026. Honeywell Aerospace (HONA) will join the S&P 500 and S&P 100 after spinning off from Honeywell International. Conagra Brands (CAG), National Health Investors (NHI), Toast (TOST), and IES Holdings (IESC) will move into various S&P indices, while Apollo Commercial Real Estate Finance (ARI), Grid Dynamics (GDYN), TopBuild (BLD), and Janus Henderson (JHG) will be removed from their current indices due to liquidation activities or acquisitions.

articles.title: Gartner Predicts Neocloud Providers Will Capture 20% of the $267B AI Cloud Market by 2030
articles.summary: Gartner forecasts that by 2030, "neocloud" providers, which specialize in AI and high-performance workloads, will secure 20% of the $267 billion AI cloud market. These providers differentiate themselves through AI-optimized infrastructure, flexible deployment models, competitive pricing, and a strong commitment to data sovereignty, challenging traditional hyperscalers. The rise of neoclouds is driven by the growing demand for GPU-intensive computing, increasing regulatory pressure for data sovereignty, and the limitations of conventional cloud models in supporting advanced AI.

articles.title: Here's How Much $1000 Invested In Bank of America 10 Years Ago Would Be Worth Today
articles.summary: An investment of $1000 in Bank of America (NYSE: BAC) made 10 years ago would be worth $4,754.52 today, based on its current price of $57.90. This demonstrates Bank of America's outperformance, yielding an average annual return of 16.38% over the decade, surpassing the market by 2.69% annually. The article highlights the significant impact of compounded returns on investment growth over time.

articles.title: How to find AI-conservative companies to work for?
articles.summary:
Is it even possible?

I don't completely hate AI, and leverage it to make things better but I don't like to use it for EVERYTHING.

At my current job we have outsourced our thinking, our design, our documentation, our sanity to it and I just can't do it anymore.

I'm considering getting out of tech entirely at this point I've been in it for almost two decades and I've reached my tipping point dealing with the way we're using it but fear basically every company hiring software engineers is doing the same thing (or worse.)

I'm ready to just be poorer at this point.
